notes:
The game runs perfectly other than not playing any music.

To get the music to work I installed a MIDI player (in my case Timidity++) and a MIDI soundfont (in my case FluidR3) and after running the Timidity service in the background the music worked perfectly fine, thus getting a perfect gameplay experience.
title: System Shock: Enhanced Edition

notes: Works perfectly fine. A tweak is required in order to get music working. For set up, install TiMidity++, FluidR3, and Daemon. Configure the TiMidity config file to use FluidR3. Start and Enable timidity.service with systemd or an alternative that you prefer to use. Before you launch the game, type in this command (without quotes): "timidity -iA" then launch the game with the Terminal running TiMidity in the background. If you prefer, you can have this command automatically executed upon start-up so you don't have to run it manually yourself every time. More details in this link: https://wiki.archlinux.org/index.php/Timidity
title: System Shock: Enhanced Edition

notes:
Update: Starting and enabling TiMidity++ to systemd makes TiMidity++ not work for SSEE until the TiMidity background process ended. Disregard the step of starting and enabling TiMidity with systemd from previous report.

Since I have been playing the game for a while, I have noticed that it gets suddenly hit with poor performance after playing the game for some time since launch. Restarting the game will make it run smooth again.
title: System Shock: Enhanced Edition

notes: There's no music (and judging from other reports, it takes more than a simple tweak to get it to work) but other than that, runs fine out of the box.
title: System Shock: Enhanced Edition

notes: I've installed Timidity and ran timidity -iA in a terminal, it got me midi. Added PULSE_LATENCY_MSEC=30 %command% to the launch options and the crackling stopped.
title: System Shock: Enhanced Edition

notes: It works fine out of the box on Proton with DXVK (DX11 to Vulkan translation), even when testing it with the new ACO compiler.
title: System Shock: Enhanced Edition
